title: The 90-Day Implementation Plan
intro:
APPENDIX B · THE OWNER’S RUNBOOK

The 90-Day Implementation Plan

Mission: Turn a shop with no system into a shop running the full HAB Sales OS in 90 days — measurably, repeatably, without burning the team out.

DURATION
90 DAYS
PHASES
3 x 30 DAYS
EXIT GATE
EVERY ADVISOR L4+
FAILURE MODE
SKIPPING WEEK 1
Overview Roles Phase 1: Stabilize Phase 2: Master Phase 3: Cement Daily / Weekly Cadence Failure Modes Day-90 Graduation

sections.title: The Three Phases — Why This Order Wins
sections.html:
The Three Phases — Why This Order Wins

Most shops that try to install a sales system fail at the same place: they try to install everything at once, the team gets overwhelmed inside two weeks, and the binder ends up in a drawer. This 90-day plan is deliberately sequenced to avoid that — each phase has a single dominant goal, and the next phase doesn’t begin until the current one is measurably won.

Figure B.1 · The three 30-day phases — one dominant goal each, gated by measurable wins

sections.title: Roles — Who Owns What
sections.html:
Roles — Who Owns What

If everyone owns the rollout, no one owns the rollout. Assign these three roles by name on Day 1 and post them on the wall. The Owner can wear two hats if necessary, but the Coach role cannot be skipped — this is where most installs die.

RoleOwnsDaily TimeSingle Most Important Behavior
Owner / GM The scoreboard. Removes blockers. Defends the cadence from operational chaos. 15–30 min Show up to the Monday standup every week. Period.
Coach / SM The drills. The ride-alongs. The 1:1s. Calibration of mastery checks. 1–2 hours Audit at least one call per advisor per day. No skips.
Advisor The reps. The self-audit worksheet. The mastery gate behaviors. 20 min self + reps in calls Fill out the self-audit worksheet every shift. Every shift.

sections.title: Phase 1 · Days 1–30 — STABILIZE
sections.html:
Phase 1 · Days 1–30 — STABILIZE

Dominant goal: Stop the most expensive leaks before installing anything else. The two biggest leaks in 95% of shops are Rapport (poorly answered phone calls, weak greetings) and Credibility (no shop tour, no warranty discipline). Fix these two and revenue moves before you’ve taught a single PRS.

WeekCurriculum FocusThis Week’s DrillsKPI Gate to Advance
Week 1 Foundation chapter. Read aloud as a team. Sign the HAB Oath. Module Key walkthrough. Trust Triangle whiteboard exercise. Greet-to-tour baseline measurement. Baseline KPIs documented. All advisors signed Oath. Roles posted on wall.
Week 2 Module 1 (Rapport) — deep. Smile-Through-Voice (1.A), The Pause (1.B), Name × 3 (1.C). Daily. 2-Ring Compliance ≥ 95%. Greet-to-Tour % improving.
Week 3 Module 2 (Credibility) — deep. The 90-Second Tour drill. Coach walks tour with every advisor at least once. Tour Delivery % = 100%. Tour → DVI Consent ≥ 90%.
Week 4 Mastery certification for Module 1 + Module 2. Boss Battle preview (Bosses 1 & 2 only). Mastery scorecard signed by Coach. All advisors certified Level 2 (Tour Guide). Phase 1 KPIs all Green.
STOPDo NOT advance to Phase 2 until every Phase-1 KPI is Green for two consecutive weeks.

The single most common failure of this rollout is sprinting through Phase 1 to “get to the good stuff.” There is no good stuff if Rapport and Credibility aren’t locked. They are the gate.

sections.title: Phase 2 · Days 31–60 — MASTER
sections.html:
Phase 2 · Days 31–60 — MASTER

Dominant goal: Install the technical sales mechanics — custody, inspection, strategy, presentation, objections, close. By the end of Phase 2, every advisor can run a full workflow, unsupervised, and hit the structural mastery checks.

WeekCurriculum FocusThis Week’s DrillsKPI Gate to Advance
Week 5 Module 3 (Custody) + Module 4 (Inspect & Analyze). Concern vs. Symptom translation drills. DVI media audit (Coach reviews 2/advisor/day). Inquiry-to-RO ≥ 70%. DVI Completion = 100%. DVI w/ Media ≥ 95%.
Week 6 Module 5 (Build & Strategize) — the Diamond. Base-order drill on every call. Anti-kitchen-sink intervention. Base-Order Compliance ≥ 90%. Same-Call Mixing ≤ 5%.
Week 7 Module 6 (Present) + the water pump worked example. PRS-on-every-finding drill. Investment-then-Coverage no-pause drill. Present → Auth ≥ 65%. PRS Completeness = 100%.
Week 8 Module 7 (Objections) + Module 8 (Close). Daily Boss Rush. Direction-Facing close drilled into muscle memory. 1st-Objection Close ≥ 55%. Authorization Rate ≥ 70%. All 8 Bosses defeated in live calls.
PRO TIPRun the Boss Rush every single morning of Phase 2.

15 minutes, all hands, standing. Coach throws random Boss lines, advisors counter in order. Score 1–3. Track the leaderboard. By Week 8 the team will have heard every Boss 40+ times in drill and they will counter in their sleep. This is the single highest-leverage Phase-2 ritual.

sections.title: Phase 3 · Days 61–90 — CEMENT
sections.html:
Phase 3 · Days 61–90 — CEMENT

Dominant goal: Turn the new behaviors into culture. Install L.A.S.T. recovery. Promote your first Champion. Make the system self-sustaining so it survives a coach being out for a week or an owner getting pulled into operations.

WeekCurriculum FocusThis Week’s DrillsKPI Gate to Advance
Week 9 Module 9 (L.A.S.T. Recovery) — deep. Silence Tolerance (9.A), BUT Buster (9.B). Coach pulls every recent complaint and runs it through the Mastery Check together. Time-to-Acknowledge ≤ 1 hr on next 5 complaints. No use of word “policy.”
Week 10 Full-workflow integration. Live ride-alongs against the complete 8-step scorecard. Each advisor gets one full ride-along. Coaching note delivered same day. Every advisor scoring 3+/5 on all 8 steps. Authorization Rate ≥ 75%.
Week 11 Mastery certification — Level 4 (Pro) for everyone who’s ready. Boss Rush final exam. Recovery scenario final exam. Owner signs Level-4 certificates. ≥ 50% of advisors at Level 4. Top advisor at Level 5 candidate.
Week 12 Culture cementing. Promote first Champion. All-team retrospective. Champion gets visible recognition (lapel pin, name on wall). Team retrospective: what to keep, what to change. Day-90 KPI snapshot beats Day-1 baseline on every metric. New rhythm has run unbroken for 12 weeks.

sections.title: The Daily & Weekly Cadence That Makes This Work
sections.html:
The Daily & Weekly Cadence That Makes This Work

The 90-day plan is built on top of a relentless weekly rhythm. Miss the rhythm one week and the install slows. Miss it two weeks and the install dies. Defend this cadence like payroll.

WhenWhoRitualTime
Mon AMAll handsStandup. Last week’s KPIs. This week’s focus metric. One team commitment.20 min
Daily AMAll hands15-min drill. Phase-appropriate (Phase 1: tour drill. Phase 2: Boss Rush. Phase 3: recovery drill.)15 min
DailyCoachAudit 1+ call per advisor. Leave one specific coaching note.30–45 min
WedCoachOne full ride-along per week. Use the Ride-Along Scorecard.45 min
End of ShiftAdvisorFill out current module’s self-audit worksheet. Two minutes.2 min
Fri PMCoach + each advisor15-min 1:1. Praise one specific behavior. Set one improvement for next week.15 min × team
Fri PMOwnerUpdate the KPI scoreboard. Color-code Green / Yellow / Red. Post visibly.15 min

sections.title: Phase-Specific Failure Modes & How to Course-Correct
sections.html:
Phase-Specific Failure Modes & How to Course-Correct

PhaseWhat Goes WrongEarly SignalCourse-Correction
Phase 1“We’re too busy for drills.”Drills skipped 2+ days in week 2.Owner attends drill in person. Public expectation reset. Drill becomes non-negotiable.
Phase 1Advisors quoting prices on phone again.Inquiry-to-Appointment dropping.Pull recordings. Replay together. Re-run Drill 1.D.
Phase 2DVIs missing media.DVI w/ Media % below 90%.Coach blocks call until media is added. One week of friction = behavior change.
Phase 2Kitchen-sinking on calls.Same-call mixing > 10%.Anti-kitchen-sink intervention (Module 5). Recorded call replay with team.
Phase 2Arguing with objections.1st-Objection Close stalled below 40%.Daily Boss Rush. Acknowledgment drill. Listen for “but.”
Phase 3Defending instead of Listening on recoveries.Time-to-Acknowledge slipping past 2 hours.Drill 9.A (Silence Tolerance). Coach personally takes the next complaint as demo.
All phasesCoach gets pulled into operations and skips audits.Coaching notes drop below 1/advisor/day.Owner blocks coach’s morning calendar. Non-negotiable. Operational fires get covered by SAs.

sections.title: Day-90 Graduation Criteria
sections.html:
Day-90 Graduation Criteria

You’ve installed the Sales OS when, on Day 90, all of the following are true. If any are not true, you are not done — add one more 30-day cycle on the specific gap and re-check on Day 120.

DAY-90 GRADUATION CHECKLIST

  • Every advisor is at Level 2 (Tour Guide) minimum; at least half are at Level 4 (Pro).
  • At least one advisor is on track for Level 5 (Champion) within 30 days.
  • All 9 KPIs on the Shop Dashboard beat their Day-1 baseline; at least 6 of 9 are Green.
  • Daily drill, weekly standup, weekly 1:1s, and weekly ride-alongs have run unbroken for 12 weeks.
  • Every advisor can deliver the shop tour, the PRS framework, and the L.A.S.T. recovery from memory.
  • The Coach can name, for each advisor, exactly which step (1–8) is their next growth area.
  • The HAB Oath is recited weekly. Posted visibly. Signed by every advisor.
  • One complaint has been recovered well enough to produce a 5-star review.

All 8 true → the shop is operating on the HAB Sales OS. Move to compounding mode (continuous mastery + new-hire onboarding).

COACH’S CORNERAfter Day 90: the compounding cycle.

Day 90 is not the finish line. It’s the moment the system becomes self-sustaining. After Day 90, shift from rollout-mode to compounding-mode: every new hire goes through a 30-day version of this same curriculum (Foundation + M1 + M2 in week 1, M3–M8 in week 2, mastery checks in week 3, full live workflows in week 4). Every existing advisor goes through one full module re-certification per quarter to prevent drift.
